commit.message:
Fix Dashboard redirecting to main app on load

Role fetch is async and completes after authLoading clears. The auth
guard was treating role=null as non-admin and redirecting to / before
the profile fetch had a chance to resolve. Now waits for role to be
explicitly set before making the access decision.

feat: package preview panel — JPEG gallery, file type breakdown, video

New GET /api/packages/[id]/preview endpoint:

  for a ready package)

  categories (RAW, EXR, JPEG, 3D Mesh, XMP, video, etc.)

    hidden gracefully)

commit.message:
feat: production-grade runtime, kernel, and CLI rewrite

Major overhaul bringing Punch from scaffolding to a working Agent OS:

Runtime (punch-runtime):
- Rewrite fighter_loop with context budget management, session repair,
  MaxTokens continuation, per-tool timeouts, and guard evaluation
- Add ContextBudget with 4-stage overflow recovery (per-result cap,
  single-result max, total headroom, trim actions)
- Add session_repair with 5-pass message validation (empty removal,
  duplicate results, orphaned results, synthetic errors, consecutive merge)
- Rewrite LoopGuard with SHA-256 hashing, ping-pong detection,
  graduated responses (Allow→Warn→Block→CircuitBreak)
- Add tools module with capability-gated tool schema resolution

Kernel (punch-kernel):
- Add BackgroundExecutor with schedule parsing, semaphore-limited
  concurrency, and watch::channel shutdown signaling
- Add WorkflowEngine for multi-step agent pipelines with variable
  substitution and OnError handling
- Add MeteringEngine with embedded price table and spend tracking
- Expand Ring with async spawn_fighter (SQLite persistence), bout
  creation, tool resolution, and graceful shutdown

CLI (punch-cli):
- Rewrite fighter commands with AgentTemplate TOML loading, model
  inheritance from global config, built-in templates (default, striker,
  scout, oracle, coder), and interactive chat REPL
- Rewrite init with Ollama auto-detection and model selection
- Rewrite start with gorilla auto-loading, PID management, Ctrl+C handler
- Rewrite doctor with Ollama/LM Studio reachability, DB checks, daemon status
- Add daemon communication helpers for all CLI commands

Validated end-to-end with local Ollama (gpt-oss:20b):
- One-shot chat, interactive REPL, tool use (file_list), daemon mode
- API endpoints: health, fighter CRUD, message exchange
- 96 tests passing, zero clippy warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: punch
repository.description: Punch — The Agent Combat System. Deploy autonomous AI agent squads (Gorillas, Chimps, Baboons) from a single Rust binary. 🦍🥊

commit.message:
Update Firebase Functions to use users collection

Updates the Cloud Functions to query the users collection instead of
the deprecated personnel collection for retrieving user display names.

Changes:
- canManageUnit: Query users collection for unit verification
- initiateTransfer: Fetch user names from users collection

This completes the migration to the merged users/personnel schema in
both client and server code.

commit.message:
Fix accessibility widget scroll and rendering bugs

- Load dyslexia font via <link> instead of @import (avoids render blocking)
- Add overscroll-behavior:contain and -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch
  to panel body to prevent scroll propagation to page
- Add wheel event handler to lock scroll inside panel at boundaries
- Prevent touch scroll from propagating to page behind panel
- Exclude widget elements from "pause animations" mode so panel
  can still open/close smoothly
- Add comprehensive CSS shield to protect widget from its own
  accessibility overrides (dark contrast, line-height, spacing, etc.)
- Switch click-outside to mousedown to avoid conflicts with buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: consolidate examples and reorganize docs (#9)

Consolidate examples from per-directory modules into single-file
examples with a shared go.mod. Reorganize docs from docs/usage/ into
docs/operations/ (per-operation reference) and docs/features/ (feature
guides). Simplify README to link to detailed docs. Update SDK import
to monorepo path. Remove emoji headings.

Tighten deck copy for VC audience, remove architecture slide

Fix mobile rendering: square grid cells and font on setup page

- Replace height/padding-top square hack with aspect-ratio: 1/1 to fix
  cell elongation on mobile (media query was adding height without
  clearing padding-top, making cells 2x taller than intended)
- Use min(33vw, 90px) and clamp() for fully responsive cell sizing
- Remove hardcoded line-height: 70px from anchor rule
- Fix setup page font-family to include UnifrakturCook (was falling
  back to generic serif on iOS/Android)

Add GCP Cloud Run Dockerfile + fix voice cut-off + slideshow + search

Docker (GCP Cloud Run):
- Multi-stage build: Node 20 Alpine for Vite build, nginx:alpine to serve
- nginx.conf.template: PORT substituted at runtime (Cloud Run sets $PORT)
- SPA fallback (try_files → index.html), asset caching, gzip
- .dockerignore excludes node_modules, dist, .git, supabase

Voice fix:
- Split recognition cleanup from speech cleanup — cancel() was firing
  on every parent re-render (isProcessing toggle), cutting speech to 2 words
- Memoize handleTranscript with stable ref so recognition effect is stable
- autoSpeak on by default; speaker = mute toggle

Historical slideshow:
- Real photos capped at 4; Gemini runs in parallel, appended as 5th slide
- Gemini-only path also returns historicalImages array for slideshow UI

Voice search:
- Expanded location triggers for natural speech (navigate to, find,
  search for, what's in/near/at, tell me about, what about)

Add WinUtil-inspired tweaks: 10 perf, 2 privacy, 5 debloater items

Performance (28→38): IPv4 preference, Teredo disable, detailed BSoD display,
End Task on taskbar, SvcHost optimization, verbose logon, show file extensions,
Explorer to This PC, disable sticky keys, notification center, mouse accel.

Privacy (23→25): Disable Consumer Features (auto-install bloat), Disable Copilot.

Debloater (22→27): Copilot, Copilot Runtime, Outlook New, Dev Home, Quick Assist.

Inspired by Chris Titus Tech WinUtil analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(m5): surrogate model CRUD + sequential auto-conclude (Phase 2) (#50)

* feat(m5): deep type-specific validation for interleaving, bandit, session, holdout experiments

- Expand validateTypeConfig() with field-level validation:
  - INTERLEAVING: method required, algorithm_ids >= 2 (>= 3 for MULTILEAVE)
  - MAB/CONTEXTUAL_BANDIT: algorithm, reward_metric_id required; exploration fraction range; context features for contextual
  - SESSION_LEVEL: session_id_attribute required
  - CUMULATIVE_HOLDOUT: is_cumulative_holdout flag consistency check
- Add validateTypeConfigForStart() for start-time reference validation (bandit reward_metric_id must exist in metric_definitions)
- 17 new unit tests, 3 integration tests with type-specific helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(m5): surrogate model CRUD — store, validation, handlers, integration tests

Implement all 4 surrogate RPCs (previously returning CodeUnimplemented):
- CreateSurrogateModel: validate + insert + audit trail
- ListSurrogateModels: keyset pagination
- GetSurrogateCalibration: get by ID
- TriggerSurrogateRecalibration: verify exists + audit trail (async via Agent-3)

New files: store/surrogate.go, store/surrogate_convert.go,
validation/surrogate.go, handlers/surrogate.go, validation/surrogate_test.go

Unblocks Agent-3 (M2.10 surrogate metric computation) and Agent-6
(surrogate projection UI).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(m5): sequential auto-conclude — Kafka consumer for boundary crossing signals

Add auto-conclude mechanism for sequential experiments (mSPRT/GST):
- Extract concludeByID from ConcludeExperiment handler for reuse
- New sequential package (consumer + processor) mirroring guardrail pattern
- Consumer listens on sequential_boundary_alerts Kafka topic
- Processor validates: experiment RUNNING + has sequential_method → auto-conclude
- Audit trail records sequential_boundary_crossed + conclude with actor=sequential_auto_conclude
- ConcludeByID public adapter on ExperimentService implements Concluder interface
- Integration test verifies full flow: start → boundary alert → auto-concluded

Unblocks Agent-4 (M4a boundary crossing → M5 auto-conclude integration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(m5): add description to sequential test SQL inserts to prevent NULL scan error

The experiments table allows NULL description, but ExperimentRow.Description
is a non-pointer string. Raw SQL inserts in sequential processor tests
omitted description, causing ListRunning to fail with "cannot scan NULL
into *string" when stream tests ran concurrently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

404 page: show concrete trustless exec hint

Derive subdomain and domain from the requested host to show
actionable commands (e.g. `trustless exec my-app`). The --domain
flag is only shown when the proxy serves multiple wildcard domains,
queried live from ProviderRegistry::wildcard_domain_count().

commit.message:
Classify AllTwelveTimingCheckKinds → §31.2 [skip ci]

Clause 31.2 (Overview) contains Syntax 31-1, which defines the grammar for all twelve timing check system tasks ($setup, $hold, $setuphold, $recovery, $removal, $recrem, $skew, $timeskew, $fullskew, $period, $width, $nochange) as a unified set. The test is a parser test that verifies all twelve check kinds are recognized and classified correctly, directly exercising this syntax definition. No more specific subclause covers all twelve together — 31.3 covers only six stability-window checks and 31.4 covers only the six clock/control-signal checks.

fix: address CodeQL security alerts (#10)

- SSRF: Add URL validation (scheme check + private IP blocking) for
  user-provided URLs in stash metadata fetching
- Clear-text storage: Encode onboarding data (API keys) in
  sessionStorage using base64 via centralized helpers
- Clear-text logging: Remove auth_info from WebSocket connection logs
- DOM XSS: Validate avatar URL scheme before rendering img src
- Weak hashing: Mark SHA-256 API key hashing with usedforsecurity=False
  (appropriate for high-entropy tokens, not passwords)

feat: mini drop zone in EntityReview + export JSON col nome del documento — v1.1.6

- EntityReview: aggiunta mini drop zone visibile quando filePath === null (sessione
  ripristinata o dizionario importato). L'utente trascina il documento direttamente
  dalla schermata di revisione senza perdere le entità importate. Le entità NER
  rilevate vengono mergeate con quelle già presenti (importate hanno priorità).
- Store: nuova azione setFilePathAndMerge(filePath, newEntities) — merge atomico
  che setta filePath e unisce le entità senza sovrascrivere quelle esistenti.
- Export dizionario: il file .json propone ora il nome del documento originale
  (es. contratto-locazione.json invece di dizionario-entita.json).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CSRF cookie not sent cross-origin in production

tsx hoists all imports, so csrf.ts evaluated before NODE_ENV was set,
resulting in sameSite:'lax' + secure:false instead of 'none' + true.
Moved env setup to config/env.ts imported first to guarantee NODE_ENV
is available when all other modules load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Complete Phase 3: Remove all personnel collection references

This commit completes the database migration by updating all remaining
code that referenced the deprecated personnel collection to use the
users collection instead.

Changes:
- PersonnelDetailPage: Query users collection by userId directly
- AddPersonnelPage: Create records in users collection with merged schema
- DataPrefetchContext: Prefetch users instead of personnel
- useUnitsManagement: Update user unitId when assigning leaders
- useAssignmentRequests: Query users for display names
- useEquipment: Fetch user data from users collection
- useTransferHistory: Query users for transfer history names
- useCanManageRole: Check users collection for permission verification
- ApprovalsManagement: Update user doc with personnel fields on approval
- ApprovalsTab: Fetch user's unit from users collection

Key changes:
- personnelId now equals userId (document ID in users collection)
- isSignatureApproved replaced by approved_user role
- All personnel lookups now query users collection
- Personnel record creation replaced by user doc updates

This completes the migration. The personnel collection can now be
safely deleted after a verification period.

Related: MIGRATION_CLEANUP_CHECKLIST.md

commit.message:
Use upstream Dogtag image for PQ PKI (ML-DSA-87 already supported)

The quay.io/dogtagpki/pki-ca:latest image (11.10.0~alpha1, fc43)
already includes ML-DSA-87 support via NSS and certutil. No need to
build PKI from source — eliminates 15-30 min build step and fixes
JSS dependency resolution failures.
